S0_separation_floor.conclusion: Separation floor is the trivial 1 (distinct lattice pts); non-consecutive walk points DO sit at Cheb 1. No usable metric gap => pure packing route is DEAD: it gives only (2rho+1)^3 (cubic, useless).

the_working_reduction.identity: c(q,rho) = sum over maximal contiguous PATH-ARCs meeting B(q,rho) of arc length <= A(rho)*L(rho)
the_working_reduction.A_return_count: max # arcs; measured <=5 for all rho<=10, level-stable (L5/L6/L7)
the_working_reduction.L_sojourn: max single-arc length; measured <= ~2.5*rho (slope a<=3), level-stable
the_working_reduction.load_bearing_c_q_4.44: direct max_c(q,4)=10/10/11, c(q,5)=12/12/14 (<=12 at rho<=4.44 OK direct); FACTORED bound 5*L(4.44)~55 does NOT reach tight 12

densest_ball_nn_structure.L6_rho10_densest: c=26 = TWO path-arcs of lengths 14 and 12 (two short near-linear passes, not a packed blob)
densest_ball_nn_structure.L6_rho6_densest: c=15 = ONE arc of length 15 (a single curve segment)
densest_ball_nn_structure.picture: dense balls are a SMALL number (<=5) of short curve-like path passes => curve/1-D, exactly the target property, but as a MEASURED orbit invariant

recurrence_window_bound_DEAD.conclusion: The single-quantity bound c<=W(2rho)+1 is BOTH loose (~50-900) AND non-level-uniform (blows up). Two DIFFERENT path-arcs passing the same ball are path-far, so the min..max index window massively overcounts. Rejected.

why_the_sublemma_is_not_free.triple_free_alone: does NOT bound single-arc sojourn linearly (free triple-free walks reach >=224; box-confined can be super-linear)
why_the_sublemma_is_not_free.naive_sojourn_renormalization: L_{k+1}(rho) <= wordlen_max * L_k(rho*||Minv||inf) = 5 * L_k(4rho/9); multiplier 5*(4/9)=2.222 > 1 => EXPANSIVE, does not close in worst case (with typical wordlen 3.4 it is 1.51, still >1)
why_the_sublemma_is_not_free.net: Level-uniformity of (A<=5, L<=arho) is strongly empirical & stable across 3 levels but its PROOF is the SAME hard renormalization kernel as the rest of L4'; Route 3 recasts the incidence bound, does not independently discharge it.
